steps.title: Spot the central contrast
steps.content: The passage directly compares sermons to printed books. Sermons are described as temporary—“a sound of words spoken to the ear...extends no farther than the strength of memory can convey it”—while books are described as permanent—“a record...conveys its contents for ages to come, to the eternity of mortal time.” The decisive clue is this contrast in durability.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer states the core comparison: print is more permanent than speech.

The passage makes exactly this point—sermons fade with memory, but books endure across generations and can be consulted as evidence or reference.

The other three choices either reverse the relationship, introduce factors the passage doesn't discuss, or misidentify the passage's central comparison.

That confirms D is correct.
